title: invitations — regulatory compliance
description: Compliance controls this module produces evidence for. Invitations are directly-solicited communications, which is the lawful basis under GDPR Art. 6(1)(a) (consent) OR Art. 6(1)(f) (legitimate interest — contractual context of workspace onboarding) — evidence for both bases is retained on the Invitation + InvitationEvent rows. When invitees are minors (guardian invitations for athletes under the age of consent), COPPA + GDPR Art. 8 apply additionally.

controls.evidence: InvitationEvent.event=`clicked` with actor_type=`invitee` = the invitee affirmatively acknowledged the invitation link. Absence of that event before accept = manual admin abuse (auditable).
Invitation.inviter_type + inviter_id + role_key establish the workspace-onboarding context. Rate limits per-invitee (5/week) prevent abuse.
POST /api/invitations/{token}/decline is always available before accept + writes InvitationEvent event=`declined`.

Consumer accept handlers can enforce this — WorkspaceInvitationAcceptHandler (owned by user module) checks the invitee's stated date-of-birth on the accept form and gates through the safeguarding module's guardian-consent flow when age < 16.
Invitation + InvitationEvent rows for a given email are exportable via the compliance module's DSAR orchestrator. Includes bounce_reason, delivered_at timestamps, and every event ip_address.

retention.json → erasureOnRequest P0D. On subject request, email is replaced with `erased-<hash>@invalid.local`, message + metadata purged, InvitationEvent rows anonymised (ip/user_agent zeroed but event + occurred_at preserved for aggregate deliverability).
Mail transports (SendGrid/SES/Postmark/Mailgun) require TLS for outbound SMTP. Enforced by transport config.

invitations.rate_limit_exceeded.total metric + webhook.signature_failed.total metric alerts route to on-call for abuse investigation. WorkspaceSecurityBreachNotification fires when platform-admin revoke is flagged isAbuseResponse=true.
See gdpr regime.

Guardian invitations for athletes under 13 (in US school + academy workspaces) route through the safeguarding module's VPC flow — the invitations module surfaces the flag `requires_vpc=true` on the accept preflight when the target invitation's role_key implies a minor-adjacent relationship.
InvitationNotification body includes a COPPA-mandated direct-notice section for guardian-of-minor invitations.
Preview page (GET /api/invitations/{token}) displays the Academorix privacy notice + workspace-specific processing purposes.

can_spam.description: Applies to any commercial e-mail messages under US law. Invitations are transactional, not commercial — CAN-SPAM exempts transactional messages from the primary requirements, but we still comply with §7704 (a)(1) (accurate header info) + §7704 (a)(2) (accurate subject line) + §7704 (a)(3) (functional return address).

controls.evidence: InvitationNotification uses the workspace's brand + address as From, with `academorix.app` sending-domain visible in Return-Path.
InvitationNotification footer contains the workspace's postal address (from WorkspaceContact where kind=billing OR the Academorix corporate address as fallback).

An invitation-to-join is implied consent under CASL where the invitee has an existing relationship with the workspace. Rate limits (5/week per invitee) + easy decline flow satisfy the additional CASL requirements.
InvitationNotification email CTA button carries the target label + inviter name so the link purpose is obvious out of context.
Accept page requires explicit confirmation checkbox before submit; decline requires a follow-up confirmation.
policies.json + permissions.json — every mutation gated by a named ability + guard.
health.json + metrics.json + invitations:audit-report weekly command output.
feature-flags.json → invitations.message-templates flag; template changes ship behind a rollout flag.
